headline: KTM Komuter Reliability Falls to 78% On-Time Performance
context: KTM Komuter on-time performance dropped to 78% in 2025 the lowest since 2018 affecting 120,000 daily commuters.

cards.big: KTM Komuter on-time rate dropped to 78% lowest in 7 years
cards.sub: 120,000 daily commuters face average delays of 22 minutes on aging system

cards.big: Fleet of 48 EMU trains has average age of 28 years with 12 out of service
cards.sub: Spare parts for 1990s trains sourced from discontinued lines at premium cost

cards.big: Track infrastructure dates from 1885 with only 35% upgraded to modern standards
cards.sub: Speed restrictions on 60% of track limit trains to 60km/h versus 120km/h design

cards.big: New 38-train EMU procurement worth RM3.2 billion approved delivery starts 2028
cards.sub: Two-year gap means aging fleet must continue beyond recommended service life

cards.big: Reliability decline tests whether deferred maintenance creates a self-reinforcing crisis
78% on-time performance for KTM Komuter. Reliability below 90% drives commuters back to private vehicles.
